tests.name: init_sh_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh exited 1 because the host-aware repo gate (--other host) requires a successful push to the fake remote URL https://example.com/fake.git, which fails by design. Project skeleton was nonetheless fully created (CLAUDE.md, .claude/, .github/workflows/, scripts/, etc.). All downstream Section A artifact assertions pass.

tests.details: current_phase=0 at init

tests.details: .github/workflows/ci.yml created (typescript)

tests.details: .github/workflows/release.yml created (web)

tests.details: .claude/framework directory present (CDF v4.2.3)

tests.name: context7_detection
tests.details: verify-install.sh reports Context7 MCP installed and configured

tests.name: platform_module_present
tests.details: docs/platform-modules/web.md present

tests.name: no_residual_backup_dir
tests.details: .claude-backup not present (BUG-002 regression check passed; init.sh removes the CDF backup post-install)

tests.name: pre_commit_gate_registered
tests.details: .claude/settings.json PreToolUse hooks include pre-commit-gate.sh

tests.name: stop_checklist_registered
tests.details: .claude/settings.json Stop hook includes stop-checklist.sh (via .claude/framework/hooks/)

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode for UAT — heredoc driver fragile
framework_bugs.reproduction: Pipe canned answers via heredoc to bash $FRAMEWORK/init.sh; flow works but exits 1 on host=other path because push to fake remote https://example.com/fake.git fails. There is no --non-interactive / --project / --platform flag.
framework_bugs.expected: init.sh accepts a manifest/flags for unattended runs, OR a --skip-remote-push flag for fake-remote testing.
framework_bugs.actual: Only --dry-run and --help flags exist; full interactive prompt walk required, and host=other forces a push that aborts the script with exit 1 even though all project files are produced.

documentation_gaps.gap: RUNBOOK Section A documents conceptual flags (--non-interactive, --project, etc.) that don't exist; agents must reverse-engineer the prompt order from init.sh source.
No documented procedure for testing init.sh against a fake remote without push failure aborting the script.

tests.name: start_phase1_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase1 OK

tests.name: phase1_steps_array_present
tests.details: .phase1_architecture.steps_completed is an array

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 PHASE1_STEPS (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed sequentially via --complete-step

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh exits 0 (vacuous since current_phase still 0; gate logic is artifact-presence based, not transition-trigger)

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: phase-state.json .current_phase did NOT auto-advance after phase1 steps + gate. Manual mutation of current_phase was required to test downstream phases.

framework_bugs.summary: No documented mechanism advances phase-state.current_phase after phase1 steps
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run --start-phase1, complete all 5 PHASE1_STEPS, run check-phase-gate.sh; current_phase stays at 0.
framework_bugs.expected: Either an explicit --advance-phase command or auto-advance when gate passes + APPROVAL_LOG entry recorded.
framework_bugs.actual: No script in scripts/ updates .current_phase except init (sets 0) and intake-wizard/upgrade-project. The runbook assertion 'phase_state_advances_to_2 after gate passes and approval is logged' has no callable code path.

documentation_gaps.gap: How does .current_phase advance from 1→2, 2→3, 3→4? RUNBOOK lists the assertion but no script reference. process-checklist.sh has no --advance-phase verb.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/process-checklist.sh (no advance verb), RUNBOOK Section B

tests.name: start_feature_records_state
tests.details: After --start-feature 'uat-feat-1', .build_loop.feature='uat-feat-1'

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: All 6 BUILD_LOOP_STEPS completed sequentially with stub artifacts (security audit md, test+impl files)

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit succeeded after all 5 required steps (commit 712dcad)

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: When build_loop.feature=null and current_phase>=2, pre-commit-gate.sh delegating to process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message returns deny JSON for 'feat(...)' messages. Verified by piping {"command":"git commit -m \"feat(...): ...\""} to scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: With .claude/pending-approval.json present, pre-commit-gate.sh emits deny JSON with the rich reason block (question/options/recommendation/offered_at).

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: After removing .claude/pending-approval.json, sentinel block stops firing; subsequent denials come from unrelated workflow gates (phase2 init verify), confirming sentinel resolved cleanly.

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: .features_completed in build-progress.json contains 'uat-feat-1'

framework_bugs.summary: pre-commit-gate.sh reads .command but Claude Code sends .tool_input.command
framework_bugs.reproduction: echo '{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"git commit ..."}}' | bash scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh — exits 0 silently; with the legacy shape '{"command":"git commit ..."}' it correctly emits deny JSON.
framework_bugs.expected: Script extracts command from .tool_input.command (with .command as fallback) per current Claude Code PreToolUse hook payload schema.
framework_bugs.actual: scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh:20 uses 'jq -r .command // empty', so the modern hook payload is silently swallowed and ALL commit gating (BL-006, BL-015, force-push, --no-verify, no-remote, etc.) is bypassed at runtime when invoked through the registered Claude Code PreToolUse hook.

framework_bugs.summary: scripts/pending-approval.sh referenced but not copied into projects
framework_bugs.reproduction: Project tree after init.sh has no scripts/pending-approval.sh, but pre-commit-gate.sh and process-checklist.sh tell users to run 'scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve'.
framework_bugs.expected: init.sh copies scripts/pending-approval.sh into the project alongside other utility scripts.
framework_bugs.actual: init.sh:1018-1037 explicitly enumerates utility scripts to copy; pending-approval.sh is missing from the list. Resolving the BL-015 sentinel via the documented command fails with 'No such file'.

tests.name: start_phase3_exits_zero
tests.details: OK with warning about UAT-features-since-last-session

tests.name: start_uat_creates_session
tests.details: .uat_session.started_at set to ISO timestamp

tests.name: uat_template_lints_clean
tests.details: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh exits 0 on both the template (warns about unreplaced placeholders) and the per-platform reference (warns about no scenarios block); exit code is 0 in both cases per the spec

tests.name: each_uat_step_completes
tests.details: All 9 UAT_STEPS completed sequentially

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh exits 0; warns about missing review manifest and deferred Phase 3 tools (license-checker, OWASP ZAP) — both expected for sponsored_poc per design

documentation_gaps.gap: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh is not copied into the project by init.sh; agents must invoke it from the framework path. Acceptable for now but should be documented.

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: EXPECTED BLOCK for sponsored_poc: --start-phase4 prints '[FAIL] Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in sponsored poc mode' and exits 0 (advisory). The block is correct per the framework's POC contract.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: N/A for sponsored_poc — phase 4 entry blocked. To exercise the steps, an agent must first run scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production. This is exactly the sponsored_poc design.

tests.name: handoff_md_required
tests.details: N/A pre-upgrade; gate logic for phase4_release:handoff_written checks for HANDOFF.md per process-checklist.sh:339 (verified by reading the case statement).

tests.name: release_notes_required
tests.details: N/A pre-upgrade; gate logic for phase4_release:go_live_verified checks for RELEASE_NOTES.md per process-checklist.sh:347.

tests.name: phase4_completion_state
tests.details: current_phase remains 4 (manually set); for sponsored_poc the framework intentionally prevents phase4 step completion until upgrade. State integrity preserved.

documentation_gaps.gap: RUNBOOK Section E does not branch on poc_mode; for sponsored_poc the entire section is N/A (or requires Section F upgrade first), but the runbook treats Section E as universally applicable for base agents.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: grep for password|api[_-]?key|secret|token in committed files returned 0 plausible-secret hits (only template/placeholder mentions)

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — no FORCE/reset events occurred during this run, so .claude/process-audit.log correctly does not exist

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles
tests.details: find .claude -name '*.tmp' returned no results

summary: Sponsored POC web/standard base run: init produces a complete project skeleton, all phase 1-3 step gates work, BL-006/BL-015 gates fire correctly when invoked with the legacy hook payload, sponsored_poc correctly blocks phase 4 entry. Two High bugs found: pre-commit-gate.sh reads the wrong JSON path (.command instead of .tool_input.command) so all PreToolUse commit gating is silently bypassed at runtime; and init.sh exits 1 on the host=other path because pushing to the fake remote fails (workaround: project files are still produced). One Medium: scripts/pending-approval.sh is referenced everywhere but not copied into projects.
